trigger_keywords: aroma a col hervida o huevo podrido
qué causa los aromas de reducción en vinos tintos

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Wine develops under very low oxygen conditions, or yeast becomes stressed during fermentation, for example when yeast-assimilable nitrogen is insufficient.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Stressed yeast can produce hydrogen sulfide, while low-oxygen conditions can allow volatile sulfur compounds to persist or accumulate.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can smell of rotten egg, cooked cabbage, rubber, or struck match, depending on the compound.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The wine can smell of rotten egg, cooked cabbage, rubber, or struck match, depending on the compound.
